name: Suspicious Activity Followed by Potential Initial Access Task
notes: This rule triggers when potential initial access tasks are performed, followed by suspicious activity. Initial access tasks include: account discovery, turning off firewall, and more.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potential Initial Access Tasks match at least 1 times in 10 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Endpoint Activities match with the same Machine Identifier (custom)
conditions_text: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potential Initial Access Tasks match at least 1 times in 10 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Endpoint Activities match with the same Machine Identifier (custom)
thresholds: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potential Initial Access Tasks match at least 1 times in 10 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Endpoint Activities match with the same Machine Identifier (custom)
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Activity Followed by Potential Initial Access Task on events which are detected by the LOCAL system

AND when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potential Initial Access Tasks match at least 1 times in 10 minutes after any of BB:BehaviorDefinition: Suspicious Endpoint Activities match with the same Machine Identifier (custom)
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Activity Followed by Endpoint Administration task

Event description: Detects a suspicious activity (communication with a potential hostile host, process masquerading, password reconnaissance, etc) is followed by a regular administration task (account management, privilege escalation, etc).
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using ProcessName (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
